case_number: constitution from the hands of respondent No.6

chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR. C. P. No.S-28 of 2025. DATE OF HEARING ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF HON’BLE JUDGE 1. For orders on office objection ‘A’. 2. For hearing of main case. 03.03.2025 Mr. Badaruddin Memon, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Shaharyar Awan, Asstt. A. G, Sindh. -.-.-.-.-.- Through this petition, petitioner Nadir Ali seeks following relief(s): a) That this Hon’abl;e Court may be pleased to direct the respondents No.6 and 10 not to cause havoc and harassment to the petitioner and his family members at the instance of private respondent. b) That this Hon’able Court may be pleased to direct the respondents No.2 to 5 to provide the protection to the petitioner, his family members and other villagers according to law and constitution from the hands of respondent No.6 to 10 and other their subordinate staff. c) That this Hon’ble Court may be pleased to call the respondents No.6 to 10 in person before this Hon’able Court and to enquire about their illegal act committed with the petitioner and further bound down them not to make any hindrance into the peaceful lives of the petitioner and his family members in future. Official respondents No.3 & 4 have filed their comments. In the comments, the official respondents have categorically stated that there are number of cases registered against the brother, father and other family members of the petitioner. It has also been mentioned that the petitioner himself is involved in a case bearing FIR No.03/2022 under section 337-A(i), F-(i), 147, 148, PPC registered at Police Station Kamal Dero. Learned Asstt. A. G submits that the petitioner only to

A. G submits that the petitioner only to 2 save himself and other family members from the clutches of law, has filed instant petition; however, he submits that the official respondents shall act strictly in accordance with law. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that he would not press instant petition if the official respondents may be directed to act strictly in accordance with law. Order accordingly. Instant petition stands disposed of. Judge M.Yousuf Panhwar/**
